nodes.summary: Audited the WAH4P repository and saved a clear project snapshot for future work. The repo is split into a NestJS backend, a Flutter frontend, and supporting SQL/docs. The backend is a secured API with global API-key protection, throttling, strict env validation, Supabase auth/data integration, and Resend email delivery. The frontend is a Flutter mobile app with centralized theming, secure local storage, a startup gate, and MPIN/session lifecycle handling. The docs describe a Supabase/Postgres source of truth for patient data, with frontend screens mostly acting as presentation and interaction layers. I also captured the current testing surface and a couple of documentation mismatches that future work should reconcile.

affectedFiles.reason: Contains repository-wide operating rules, architecture reminders, and documentation guidance that shape how the project should be understood.
affectedFiles.notes: Established the backend/frontend responsibility split and project conventions.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines the canonical data model, storage layers, and screen-to-data mapping used by the app.
affectedFiles.notes: Important for understanding persistence and source-of-truth rules.

affectedFiles.reason: Lists the current persistence and validation work items across the app and acts as a project readiness tracker.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for identifying unfinished foundation work.

affectedFiles.reason: Documents the screen inventory and route mapping, which is critical for understanding the frontend surface area.
affectedFiles.notes: Contains a likely path mismatch with the live repo layout.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the backend stack, scripts, test tooling, and key runtime dependencies.
affectedFiles.notes: Confirms NestJS, Supabase, Resend, Jest, and validation tooling.

affectedFiles.reason: Summarizes backend setup, auth endpoints, SQL bootstrap, and local development steps.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for capturing the intended API surface.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows backend bootstrap behavior, including security middleware, route prefixing, validation, and CORS configuration.
affectedFiles.notes: Establishes the HTTP entrypoint behavior.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines the backend module composition, including global guards and configuration wiring.
affectedFiles.notes: Confirms API-key and throttling guards are global.

affectedFiles.reason: Lists the required environment variables and validation constraints for the backend.
affectedFiles.notes: Critical for deployment and security readiness.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows how the backend enforces the x-api-key header and compares secrets safely.
affectedFiles.notes: Important security boundary for all non-public routes.

affectedFiles.reason: Documents runtime configuration for the Flutter client and how it connects to the backend.
affectedFiles.notes: Confirms the app uses backend URL and API key configuration.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the Flutter SDK version, package dependencies, and bundled assets used by the app.
affectedFiles.notes: Confirms secure storage, HTTP, QR, and local asset usage.

affectedFiles.reason: Captures the Flutter lint baseline for the client codebase.
affectedFiles.notes: No custom lint overrides beyond the default Flutter lint set.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the Flutter application entrypoint and how the app widget is launched.
affectedFiles.notes: Thin entrypoint that delegates to the app shell.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines the Flutter app shell, theme, route generation, startup gate, and MPIN lock-on-resume behavior.
affectedFiles.notes: Central file for app lifecycle and navigation policy.

nodes.summary: Saved a deeper project map for WAH4P that focuses on navigation, feature boundaries, and service responsibilities. The repository presents a clear two-tier architecture: a NestJS backend that owns auth/security/business rules and a Flutter frontend that owns display, navigation, and device-scoped state. The frontend route tree now has a precise map from startup through onboarding, auth, dashboard, PHR, EHR-style records, appointments, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. The backend API surface is also clearer: a public health check plus a broad auth/profile domain that covers registration, login, 2FA, MPIN, password reset, and profile hydration/update. This memory layer is intended to function as an app map, not just a summary.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines the Flutter app shell, theme, route entrypoint, and resume-time lock behavior, which are central to the app map.
affectedFiles.notes: Confirms where app-wide navigation and lifecycle control live.

affectedFiles.reason: Contains the full named route registry and screen-to-route mapping for the Flutter client.
affectedFiles.notes: This is the best source of truth for the app’s visible navigation tree.

affectedFiles.reason: Lists every canonical route constant used by the Flutter app.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for maintaining route names and screen grouping.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows how the app waits for startup resolution and then redirects to the correct initial route.
affectedFiles.notes: Important for understanding the launch flow.

affectedFiles.reason: Implements startup routing decisions from auth, onboarding, and MPIN state.
affectedFiles.notes: This is the decision engine behind splash-to-app routing.

affectedFiles.reason: Implements the app’s background timing and unlock requirement policy.
affectedFiles.notes: Key to understanding resume/lock behavior.

affectedFiles.reason: Directory structure reveals the major presentation feature families that make up the client.
affectedFiles.notes: Confirms the app is organized by domain feature.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines backend module composition and global guards, which frame the server-side architecture map.
affectedFiles.notes: Shows the core backend wiring.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows how the backend auth domain is composed from controllers, services, and repositories.
affectedFiles.notes: Critical for understanding auth responsibility split.

affectedFiles.reason: Lists the auth workflow endpoints and groups them into registration, login, 2FA, security, and MPIN routes.
affectedFiles.notes: Primary backend workflow surface.

affectedFiles.reason: Exposes profile hydration and update endpoints separate from the auth workflow controller.
affectedFiles.notes: Important for profile screen integration.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides the public health endpoint and marks the small public server surface.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for operational checks.

nodes.summary: Added a plain-English atlas for WAH4P so future work can read the project like a map. The repo is best understood in layers: a Flutter client that controls navigation and presentation, a NestJS backend that owns security and business rules, and Supabase/Postgres plus secure local storage for persistence. The most important user journeys now have an explicit flow: splash/startup -> onboarding or login -> dashboard or MPIN unlock -> feature areas like records, appointments, resupply, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. This memory also clarifies which screens are static, which are route-driven, and which backend APIs support each major journey.

affectedFiles.reason: The app shell controls theme, startup gating, and resume-time locking, making it the top-level navigation anchor for the project map.
affectedFiles.notes: Central entry point for the Flutter client.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines the route decision logic for onboarding, login, dashboard, and MPIN unlock, which is essential to the launch map.
affectedFiles.notes: Startup flow source of truth.

affectedFiles.reason: Lists the canonical route names used throughout the Flutter app.
affectedFiles.notes: Best place to understand route coverage.

affectedFiles.reason: Maps route names to screens and shows transition style choices for each user flow.
affectedFiles.notes: Best source for the screen-to-route atlas.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the transient gate screen that hands off to the resolved initial route during startup.
affectedFiles.notes: Launch UX and routing handoff.

affectedFiles.reason: Implements the time-based unlock policy used when the app resumes from background.
affectedFiles.notes: Important for security and session behavior.

affectedFiles.reason: The feature directory structure reveals the major product areas and how the client is partitioned by domain.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for understanding the app map by feature group.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the backend’s top-level composition, including global guards and core modules.
affectedFiles.notes: Server architecture anchor.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the backend auth domain composition across controllers, services, and repositories.
affectedFiles.notes: Primary auth subsystem map.

affectedFiles.reason: Exposes the auth workflow endpoints and groups them into clear account/security journeys.
affectedFiles.notes: API surface map for auth.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides authenticated profile read/update endpoints separate from the workflow controller.
affectedFiles.notes: Profile API map.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the public health check route and the small public surface of the server.
affectedFiles.notes: Operational entry point.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines persistence rules, canonical entities, and the source-of-truth model used by the app.
affectedFiles.notes: Data/storage map.

affectedFiles.reason: Tracks unfinished persistence and validation work across the product and shows which areas still need implementation.
affectedFiles.notes: Project readiness map.

affectedFiles.reason: Documents screen inventory and route flow, though it is older and should be reconciled with the live code.
affectedFiles.notes: Secondary reference, not authoritative.

nodes.summary: Fixed the Security Settings screen crash caused by calling ScaffoldMessenger.of(context) during initState-driven async hydration. The login redirect is now deferred with a post-frame callback, which keeps the cached preloaded Security screen behavior intact and avoids inherited-widget timing errors. Flutter analysis passed after the fix.

affectedFiles.reason: Changed the login redirect to run after the first frame so the screen no longer throws when initState-triggered async work calls ScaffoldMessenger too early.
affectedFiles.notes: The rest of the cache-first loading behavior remains intact.

nodes.summary: Updated the profile personal information screen to visually align with the BMI screen's card-based layout and header spacing, while preserving the existing profile hydration and save/reset behavior. Also updated the widget test to reflect the new card sections and verified the feature with flutter test and flutter analyze.

affectedFiles.reason: Restyled the screen to match the BMI screen's layout and card treatment while preserving its profile edit behavior.
affectedFiles.notes: Added private header, summary card, and form card widgets inside the file.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the widget test to reflect the new card-based rendering and helper text on the personal information screen.
affectedFiles.notes: Kept the same functional assertions for title, fields, and action buttons.

nodes.summary: Changed the profile personal information screen from a card-based layout to a flatter BMI-like page structure after the user clarified they wanted no card styling. The screen now uses a plain header, inline profile summary row, divider separation, and flat form sections while keeping the existing save/reset behavior intact.

affectedFiles.reason: Removed the card-like shells and replaced them with a flat header, summary row, divider, and form section.
affectedFiles.notes: Kept the behavior and compatibility parameters unchanged.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the test description to reflect the flat layout while preserving the main content assertions.
affectedFiles.notes: No functional assertions were removed.
